title: Parallax dispatched-subagent manifest (v0.38 F8)
description: Per-slug machine record of every subagent track dispatched for a run, at .parallax/<slug>/subagents.json. Written AT DISPATCH (commands/run.md Step 2a) and updated as tracks report, then committed to feature/<slug> so it survives a session boundary and a fresh cloud clone (same rule as the review ledgers). This is the enabling artifact for /parallax:run --adopt: without it, a run that died mid-build with in-flight BACKGROUND tracks has no machine record of what was dispatched, on what branch, expecting what commit, in which session — exactly what RUN2's operator had to reconstruct by hand into RUN-HANDOFF.md. Not a benchmark and not a substitute for the arbiter/verifier receipts; it records WHAT WAS DISPATCHED, never that a slice is verified.

run_id.description: Must equal the run-state / run-evidence run_id — a manifest cannot be filed under another run.

entries.description: One entry per (slice, role) track. A re-dispatch of the same (slice, role) UPDATES its entry in place (latest branch/tip/status), never appends a duplicate — so the manifest always names the current in-flight state.

role.description: Which subagent was dispatched. The two IN-FLIGHT tracks adopt reaps are test-writer and blind-coder; arbiter/codex-judge are recorded for a complete dispatch trail.

branch.description: The branch this track commits to (e.g. feature/<slug>-S6-code / -test in parallel, feature/<slug>-code / -test sequential). Adopt resolves the ACTUAL tip from git; this records what was EXPECTED.

wave_base.description: The integration tip this track forked from — a track branch whose live tip is a descendant of wave_base carries real work and is reap-eligible; a tip equal to wave_base carries none.

session_id.description: The session that dispatched this track — so adopt (a DIFFERENT session) knows the completion notification cannot have crossed the boundary.

mode.description: background = dispatched as a background agent whose completion notification does NOT cross a session boundary (the RUN2 gap adopt reconstructs from git); foreground = awaited in-session.

status.description: dispatched = launched, no report yet; reported = the track reported its done-gate in-session; reaped = adopt read a real commit off its branch to REPLACE the missed cross-session notification; stale = the recorded branch no longer exists (never silently trusted — a stale entry is drift, not a green).

reported_commit.description: The commit the track was last known to have produced (from an in-session report, or read off git by adopt when reaped). null until known — never invented.
